title: prestonia erecta
intro: Prestonia erecta, also known as "Santa Maria" or "Ipecacuanha", is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its emetic properties, which induce vomiting to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Prestonia erecta are attributed to its alkaloid compounds, including ipecac, which have been used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Prestonia erecta include tinctures, infusions, and powdered root forms, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including nausea, indigestion, and coughs.
